name: tsl_partial_name_ambiguity
description: [TSL S5] Partial-name ambiguity: two distinct objects sharing a common token are both established in turn 1; player uses the generic shared token as the acquisition target. TSL alias resolution must annotate unresolved_ambiguity when multiple ObjectRecords match.

tsl_expected.notes: Two tracked objects share the token 'cup': 'red clay cup' and 'blue ceramic cup', both established in the founding turn. Player input says 'pick up the cup' — ambiguous. If CB emits a candidate named 'cup', TSL token_subset matching finds both records match. TSL must annotate unresolved_ambiguity (it cannot determine which cup the player means) rather than silently resolving to an arbitrary match. alias_candidate entry for 'cup' must have non-null unresolved_ambiguity. acquisition_signal must carry the same unresolved_ambiguity annotation. This verifies TSL's ambiguity surface is active: partial-name references with multiple possible targets must be flagged, not silently collapsed.

alias_candidates.notes: Multiple ObjectRecords match 'cup' — TSL must annotate ambiguity rather than silently picking one

world_prompt: I am in a small kitchen. On the wooden counter sit two cups side by side: a red clay cup and a blue ceramic cup.
